testCases.title: Successful login with valid credentials returns JWT and redirects to dashboard
testCases.description: Verify that a registered user can log in with correct email and password, receives a valid JWT token, and is redirected to the task dashboard.
preconditions: TaskMaster frontend is running on http://localhost:3000
TaskMaster backend is running on http://localhost:3001
A registered user account exists with email 'testuser@example.com' and password 'Password1'

steps.action: Open a browser and navigate to http://localhost:3000/login
steps.expectedOutcome: The Login page is displayed with email and password input fields and a Submit button

steps.action: Enter 'testuser@example.com' into the Email field
steps.expectedOutcome: The email value appears correctly in the input field

steps.action: Enter 'Password1' into the Password field
steps.expectedOutcome: The password is masked and accepted

steps.action: Click the Login / Submit button
steps.expectedOutcome: A POST request is sent to http://localhost:3001/api/auth/login

steps.action: Observe the HTTP response and the UI state after the request completes
steps.expectedOutcome: The API returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing 'token' and 'user' object (id, name, email); the frontend stores the token and redirects the user to the task dashboard route

steps.action: Inspect browser local storage or session storage for the JWT token
steps.expectedOutcome: A JWT token is present and non-empty in storage

testCases.expectedResult: User is authenticated, JWT is stored client-side, and the task dashboard is displayed without any error messages.
acceptanceCriteria: API responds with HTTP 200
Response body contains 'token' (non-empty string) and 'user' with correct id, name, and email
Frontend redirects to dashboard route after successful login
No error message is shown on screen

testCases.title: Authenticated user can create a new task with all required and optional fields
testCases.description: Verify that a logged-in user can create a task by providing title, description, priority, and due date, and the task appears in the task list.
preconditions: TaskMaster frontend is running on http://localhost:3000
TaskMaster backend is running on http://localhost:3001
User is logged in and on the task dashboard
A valid JWT token is stored in the browser

steps.action: Click the 'Add Task' or 'New Task' button on the dashboard
steps.expectedOutcome: A task creation form or modal is displayed with fields: Title, Description, Priority (dropdown), Due Date

steps.action: Enter 'Buy groceries' in the Title field
steps.expectedOutcome: Title field accepts and displays the text

steps.action: Enter 'Milk, eggs, bread' in the Description field
steps.expectedOutcome: Description field accepts and displays the text

steps.action: Select 'high' from the Priority dropdown
steps.expectedOutcome: 'high' is selected as priority

steps.action: Enter a future date (e.g., '2025-12-31') in the Due Date field
steps.expectedOutcome: Due date field accepts the date value

steps.action: Click the Save / Create Task button
steps.expectedOutcome: A POST request is sent to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks with Authorization header and the task payload

steps.action: Observe the API response and the updated task list on the dashboard
steps.expectedOutcome: API returns HTTP 201 with the newly created task object including a generated 'id', 'completed: false', and correct field values; the task 'Buy groceries' appears in the task list

testCases.expectedResult: New task is persisted in the database, returned with a unique ID, and displayed in the task list with correct title, priority, and due date.
acceptanceCriteria: API responds with HTTP 201
Response body contains the new task with auto-generated id, correct title, description, priority, due_date, and completed = false
Task appears in the dashboard task list immediately after creation
No error messages are displayed

testCases.title: Authenticated user can update an existing task's title and priority
testCases.description: Verify that a logged-in user can edit an existing task, change its title and priority, save the changes, and the updated values persist in the UI and database.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the task dashboard
At least one existing task is visible in the task list (e.g., task with title 'Buy groceries')

steps.action: Locate the task 'Buy groceries' in the task list and click its Edit button or title link
steps.expectedOutcome: An edit form or modal is displayed pre-populated with the task's current values

steps.action: Clear the Title field and enter 'Buy groceries and vegetables'
steps.expectedOutcome: Title field displays the new text

steps.action: Change the Priority dropdown from 'high' to 'medium'
steps.expectedOutcome: 'medium' is selected

steps.action: Click the Save / Update button
steps.expectedOutcome: A PUT request is sent to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks/:id with Authorization header and updated fields

steps.action: Observe the API response and the task list
steps.expectedOutcome: API returns HTTP 200 with the updated task object; the task list shows the new title 'Buy groceries and vegetables' with priority 'medium'

steps.action: Reload the page and locate the same task
steps.expectedOutcome: Updated title and priority are still displayed, confirming database persistence

testCases.expectedResult: Task is updated in the database with the new title and priority, and changes are reflected in the UI after reload.
acceptanceCriteria: API responds with HTTP 200
Response body contains the task with updated title and priority values
Updated values persist after a page reload
The updated_at timestamp is newer than the original created_at timestamp

testCases.title: Login fails with incorrect password and shows error message
testCases.description: Verify that attempting to log in with a valid registered email but an incorrect password returns HTTP 401 and displays an appropriate error message to the user.
preconditions: TaskMaster frontend is running on http://localhost:3000
TaskMaster backend is running on http://localhost:3001
A registered user account exists with email 'testuser@example.com'

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/login
steps.expectedOutcome: Login page is displayed

steps.action: Enter 'testuser@example.com' in the Email field
steps.expectedOutcome: Email value is accepted

steps.action: Enter 'WrongPassword99' in the Password field
steps.expectedOutcome: Password value is masked and accepted

steps.action: Click the Login / Submit button
steps.expectedOutcome: A POST request is sent to http://localhost:3001/api/auth/login

steps.action: Observe the API response and the UI
steps.expectedOutcome: API returns HTTP 401 with JSON body { error: 'Invalid email or password' }; the frontend displays an error message on screen (e.g., 'Invalid email or password')

steps.action: Check that no JWT token has been stored in browser storage
steps.expectedOutcome: No token exists in localStorage or sessionStorage; user remains on the login page

testCases.expectedResult: Login is rejected, no token is issued, user stays on the login page, and a clear error message is displayed.
acceptanceCriteria: API responds with HTTP 401
Response body contains { error: 'Invalid email or password' }
No JWT token is stored in browser storage
An error message is visible on the login page
User is not redirected to the dashboard

testCases.title: Creating a task without a title is rejected and shows a validation error
testCases.description: Verify that submitting a new task form with the Title field empty is rejected by both the frontend and the API, and the user sees an appropriate validation error.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the task dashboard
The task creation form is accessible

steps.action: Click the 'Add Task' or 'New Task' button on the dashboard
steps.expectedOutcome: Task creation form is displayed

steps.action: Leave the Title field completely empty
steps.expectedOutcome: Title field remains blank

steps.action: Enter 'low' as Priority and a valid due date
steps.expectedOutcome: Other fields are filled

steps.action: Click the Save / Create Task button
steps.expectedOutcome: Either client-side validation prevents submission and shows an inline error, OR a POST request is sent to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks

steps.action: If a request was sent, observe the API response
steps.expectedOutcome: API returns HTTP 400 with a JSON error body indicating that title is required (e.g., { error: 'Title is required' })

steps.action: Check the task list on the dashboard
steps.expectedOutcome: No new task has been added to the list

testCases.expectedResult: Task creation is blocked, no task record is created in the database, and a validation error message is shown to the user.
acceptanceCriteria: Task is not created (HTTP 400 from API, or client-side validation prevents submission)
A descriptive error message is displayed to the user
Task list count does not increase
Form remains open for the user to correct the input

testCases.title: Task title at maximum boundary length (255 characters) is accepted; 256 characters is rejected or truncated
testCases.description: Verify boundary behavior for the task title field. A title of 255 characters should be accepted, while behavior for 256 characters should be defined and consistent (rejected with error or truncated).
preconditions: User is logged in and on the task dashboard
Task creation form is accessible

steps.action: Open the task creation form
steps.expectedOutcome: Form is displayed with a Title field

steps.action: Generate a string of exactly 255 characters (e.g., repeat 'A' 255 times) and paste it into the Title field
steps.expectedOutcome: The Title field accepts the 255-character string

steps.action: Select any valid Priority and click Save
steps.expectedOutcome: POST request is sent to /api/tasks with the 255-character title

steps.action: Observe the API response
steps.expectedOutcome: API returns HTTP 201; task is created with the full 255-character title

steps.action: Open the task creation form again and enter a title of exactly 256 characters
steps.expectedOutcome: Input field either truncates to 255 characters or accepts 256 characters for submission

steps.action: Click Save and observe the response
steps.expectedOutcome: If the application enforces a 255-character limit: HTTP 400 is returned with a validation error, or client prevents submission. If no limit is enforced, document actual behavior.

steps.action: Check that a 1-character title (single letter) is also accepted
steps.expectedOutcome: API returns HTTP 201 for a single-character title; task appears in task list

testCases.expectedResult: A 255-character title is accepted and stored. Behavior for a 256-character title is consistent with application constraints (rejected or truncated). A 1-character title is always accepted.
acceptanceCriteria: Task with 255-character title is created successfully (HTTP 201)
Task with 1-character title is created successfully (HTTP 201)
Behavior for titles exceeding any documented maximum is consistent and predictable
If validation fails, HTTP 400 is returned with a descriptive error

testCases.title: Accessing task endpoints without a JWT token returns 401 Unauthorized
testCases.description: Verify that all protected task API endpoints return HTTP 401 when called without an Authorization header, ensuring unauthenticated access is blocked.
preconditions: TaskMaster backend is running on http://localhost:3001
An API client (e.g., Postman, curl, or a REST client) is available
At least one task exists in the database for GET/PUT/DELETE tests

steps.action: Send a GET request to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks with NO Authorization header
steps.expectedOutcome: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON body { error: 'Unauthorized' } or similar unauthorized message

steps.action: Send a POST request to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks with a valid task body but NO Authorization header
steps.expectedOutcome: Response is HTTP 401; no task is created in the database

steps.action: Send a PUT request to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks/1 with valid update body but NO Authorization header
steps.expectedOutcome: Response is HTTP 401; task data is not modified

steps.action: Send a DELETE request to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks/1 with NO Authorization header
steps.expectedOutcome: Response is HTTP 401; task is not deleted

steps.action: Send a GET request to http://localhost:3001/api/tasks with an Authorization header containing an expired or malformed token (e.g., 'Bearer invalidtoken123')
steps.expectedOutcome: Response is HTTP 401 with an error message indicating the token is invalid or expired

steps.action: Verify the database state after all the above requests
steps.expectedOutcome: No data was created, modified, or deleted as a result of unauthenticated requests

testCases.expectedResult: All task API endpoints consistently return HTTP 401 when called without a valid JWT token, and no data mutations occur.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tasks without token returns HTTP 401
POST /api/tasks without token returns HTTP 401 and creates no task
PUT /api/tasks/:id without token returns HTTP 401 and modifies no task
DELETE /api/tasks/:id without token returns HTTP 401 and deletes no task
An invalid or malformed token also results in HTTP 401
Response body contains an error field with a meaningful message

testCases.title: Task dashboard renders correctly and is fully usable at 480px mobile viewport width
testCases.description: Verify that the TaskMaster task dashboard layout adapts correctly to a 480px-wide mobile viewport, ensuring all key UI elements are visible, readable, and interactive without horizontal scrolling.
preconditions: TaskMaster frontend is running on http://localhost:3000
User is logged in and has at least 3 tasks in the list
Browser DevTools or a physical/emulated mobile device is available
Viewport is set to 480px width (e.g., via Chrome DevTools device emulation)

steps.action: Open Chrome DevTools (F12), go to Device Toolbar, and set the viewport to 480px wide x 850px tall
steps.expectedOutcome: Viewport is set to 480px; the page reloads or re-renders at the new size

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3000 and log in if not already authenticated
steps.expectedOutcome: Login form is displayed in a single-column layout; fields and button are full-width and easily tappable

steps.action: After login, observe the task dashboard layout
steps.expectedOutcome: Navigation header or menu is visible; if a hamburger/collapsed menu is used, the icon is visible and functional; task list is displayed in a vertical single-column format

steps.action: Scroll through the task list and check for horizontal overflow
steps.expectedOutcome: No horizontal scrollbar is present; all task cards/rows fit within the 480px width without content being clipped

steps.action: Tap or click the 'Add Task' button
steps.expectedOutcome: Task creation form opens and is fully visible and usable within the 480px viewport; all form fields are accessible without horizontal scrolling

steps.action: Create a new task by filling in Title and selecting Priority, then tapping Save
steps.expectedOutcome: Task is created successfully and appears in the task list; success feedback is visible on screen

steps.action: Tap the Edit button on an existing task card
steps.expectedOutcome: Edit form is displayed correctly within the mobile viewport; Save and Cancel buttons are accessible

steps.action: Tap the Delete button on a task and confirm deletion
steps.expectedOutcome: Task is removed from the list; confirmation dialog (if present) is fully visible and interactive at 480px

steps.action: Check font sizes, button sizes, and touch target sizes across all visible elements
steps.expectedOutcome: Text is legible without zooming; interactive elements (buttons, inputs) have a minimum touch target size of approximately 44x44px; no text overlaps

testCases.expectedResult: The full task management workflow (view, create, edit, delete) is functional and visually correct at 480px viewport width with no horizontal scrolling and adequate touch target sizes.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard layout is single-column at 480px width
No horizontal scrollbar or content overflow is present
All CRUD actions (add, edit, delete, complete) are accessible and functional
Form fields and buttons are full-width and tappable
Text is legible without user-initiated zoom
Navigation elements are accessible at mobile width
